definition: Knowing arising dependent on the body and tangibles.
notes: The project prefers body-knowing because it stays close to the canonical sixfold formula while aligning with the updated treatment of viññāṇa as knowing. Body-consciousness remains an acceptable alternate where continuity with familiar doctrinal English matters.

context_rules.context: In most analytical and source-facing contexts
In explanatory prose emphasizing the tactile mode
In comparative doctrinal prose using established Buddhist-English terminology
context_rules.notes: Acceptable alternate when continuity with standard terminology helps.

example_phrases.pali: kāyañca paṭicca phoṭṭhabbe ca uppajjati kāyaviññāṇaṃ
example_phrases.translation: dependent on the body and tangibles, body-knowing arises
